components.name: Shelter
components.source: Zillow Observed Rent Index (ZORI), national, smoothed seasonally adjusted
components.note: Real market-rate rents tracked from millions of Zillow rental listings, replacing BLS's Owners' Equivalent Rent which lags by 6-12 months. Weight matches Truflation's published 23.2% housing weight (BLS-CEX uses 33% — we believe Truflation's weight better reflects current consumer expenditure).

components.name: Food
components.note: All food (at home and away from home), urban consumers. Weight matches Truflation's 15.3%.

components.name: Utilities
components.note: Electricity costs — used as proxy for household utility costs (NOT motor fuel). Electricity is the largest single household utility expense. Motor fuel is captured inside the Transportation component instead, matching Truflation's basket structure and avoiding the double-count that occurs when motor fuel is also a separate component.

components.name: Transportation
components.source: BLS CPI: Transportation (FRED: CPITRNSL) — includes motor fuel
components.note: Vehicles, motor fuel, maintenance, insurance, public transit, airfare. BLS Transportation already includes motor fuel, so this component naturally carries our fuel exposure. Weight matches Truflation's published 19.8%.

components.name: Medical care
components.note: Insurance premiums, hospital services, prescription drugs. Weight matches Truflation's 8.5%.

components.name: Apparel
components.note: Clothing and footwear. Carved out from the broad 'core' category — apparel has been deflating since 2023, which gets washed out in BLS Core CPI.

components.name: Recreation
components.note: Sports, hobbies, media, pets, recreational services. Carved out from broad 'core' — typically grows slower than headline core.

components.name: Education & Communication
components.source: BLS CPI: Education and Communication (FRED: CPIEDUSL)
components.note: Tuition, school supplies, phone plans, internet, postage. Carved out from broad 'core' — communications subindex has been deflating, dragging this lower.

components.name: Household furnishings
components.source: BLS CPI: Household Furnishings and Operations (FRED: CUUR0000SAH3)
components.note: Furniture, appliances, kitchen, cleaning supplies, household services. Carved out from broad 'core' — durables have been broadly deflating since 2023.

components.name: Other (residual core)
components.note: Everything not captured above — alcohol, tobacco, personal care, miscellaneous services. Uses headline core CPI as a proxy.

methodology.summary: Ember True Inflation is a weighted average of year-over-year price changes across 10 consumer spending categories, modeled after Truflation's published category structure. Three methodological choices drive Ember's divergence from BLS CPI: (1) we use Zillow's Observed Rent Index (ZORI) for shelter instead of BLS's lagging Owners' Equivalent Rent; (2) we structure Energy/Transportation the way Truflation does — Utilities covers electricity (motor fuel is captured inside Transportation, avoiding the double-count that occurs when motor fuel is also a separate component); (3) we carve out apparel, recreation, education/communication, and household furnishings as separate components rather than folding them into a single 'core' category — important since tariff effects on goods are no longer uniformly deflationary.
methodology.weights_source: Truflation 2026 published weights where defensible (housing 23%, transportation 19%, food 15%, medical 8%), with sub-category carve-outs to expose goods inflation that gets averaged away in broad core indices
methodology.data_sources: Zillow Research + Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), St. Louis Fed
methodology.update_frequency: Daily. Component data updates monthly; we recompute daily to pick up any revisions.
